ruleArray.label: You meet the minimum TX State GPA requirement
Complete Core for College of Science and Engineering Students

titleList: Major in Computer Science (Bachelor of Science)

textList: All first-time undergraduate students must show foreign language proficiency. Documentation is collected by Undergraduate Admissions to ensure this requirement is met.
Students must complete either two years of the same foreign language in high school or two semesters in college to meet this requirement.
Acceptable documentation could include high school transcripts, or college transcripts.
To see a list of the General Education Core Curriculum go to the following website: http://mycatalog.txstate.edu/undergraduate/general-education-core-curriculum/
Note: Although courses may meet core areas for core curriculum requirements, certain core courses may also be required to satisfy specific degree/major requirements.
Students may have to take additional classes if the core course they completed didn't satisfy their degree/major requirements.
See your academic advisor for further clarification.

ruleArray.label: Six credits with Core Code 010 are required - the CS major requires a grade of C or better in at least one of these English courses
The core Mathematics requirement (Core Code 020 - MATH 2471) will be satisfied via the major
The core Life and Physical Sciences requirement (Core Code 030) will be satisfied via the major.
The Language, Philosophy, and Culture requirement (Core Code 040) will be satisfied via the major (PHIL 1305 or 1320)
Six credits combined from Core Code 091 (COMM 1310) and Core Code 092 (MATH 2472) will be satisfied in the major

blockArray.title: Major in Computer Science (Bachelor of Science)

textList: In addition to satisfying the University graduation requirements, students must earn a grade of C or higher in all computer science,
English, and mathematics courses used to satisfy the requirements of the computer science major.
